title: trond apply output
description: Returned by `trond apply --intent ... -o json`. Describes the deployment outcome and the live endpoints, when --wait succeeded.

name.description: The intent.name, also the managed-node identifier in state.json.

result.description: no_change means the intent matches what's already deployed (idempotent re-run); created on first apply; updated when the intent or its rendered config differs from state and --auto-approve was passed.

intent_hash.description: SHA256 of the intent file contents. Used by the next apply to detect changes.

config_hash.description: SHA256 of the rendered HOCON config. Same intent → same config_hash unless template changed.

endpoints.description: Live API endpoints exposed by the running container. Keys vary by node type and which features are enabled.

duration_ms.description: Apply wall-clock time in milliseconds (excludes --wait polling).

waited_ms.description: Wait wall-clock time when --wait was passed; only present when --wait was set.

ready.description: True if --wait was set AND the node's HTTP API responded before the timeout. Absent when --wait was not requested.

wait_error.description: Set when --wait timed out or the probe failed. Apply itself succeeded; the node was deployed but did not become ready in time. Caller should call `trond diagnose <name>` to investigate.

version.description: Resolved java-tron image / jar version that got deployed.

runtime.description: Which runtime was selected based on intent.target.runtime.

build.description: Populated when the intent carried a `build:` block (spec/002). Slice of the full build.Result manifest with the fields a caller most often needs inline. The complete manifest stays under ~/.trond/builds/manifest/<cache_key>.json.

builder_image.description: Pinned JDK builder image reference (e.g. eclipse-temurin:8-jdk-jammy@sha256:...) used to produce this artifact.
